hard.next_step: 6/26 BCE: Reached out to Nicole, SVP of IT 6/17 Going to offer a live netsuite integration 6/15 BCE: In limbo. Someone from the Conga team viewed the demo. 5/30 BCE: Andy has gone quiet. Still pushing 5/8 BCE: Demo occurred. Went well. Following up with Andy on next steps. Seems like implementation/integration call is next step 5/1 BCE: RFP Submitted. Demo next week 4/20 BCE: Met with Andy again. Gained requirements. RFP is due end of April. Demo to follow. 4/14 RFP is out and we are in process 3/16 BCE: Awaiting 2/23 BCE: Signed NDA. RFP should be released in a week or two 1/8/26 BCE: Reaching out next week to ask about RFP 12/26 BCE: Met with Andy; Sr Dir of Procurement (New Hire). Sending out a NDA to issue the RFP. Want to make a decision by March. Currently Acquiring a company and will finalize in Feb
hard.ais_why:
• Even though Merlin isn't in the explicit product scope and the RFP carries no AI rating, the May 2026 demo captured clear buyer-stated AI enthusiasm. Procurement lead Andrew Simmons praised the agentic negotiation feature as awesome, and Craig Adler asked directly: "Within the workflow, can you deploy an agent?" Those are buyer-initiated agentic questions.
• Craig also described the Merlin Intake use case unprompted: "Can you populate an intake for me and figure out what category I'm buying?" Ida Stark and Maria Murillo backed that up with engagement on Smartdesk invoice automation, and Bailey Erazo committed to share Zylow integration details and ballpark pricing, signalling real evaluation traction.
• Why not Hungry: the explicit in-scope set is iContract, iSource, and iSupplier with no Merlin or ANA module committed, the September go-live is targeted at an MVP NetSuite plus AP integration (not AI), and risks include supplier portal adoption that's unlikely to hit 100%. Buyer enthusiasm is real but not yet anchored to a paid AI workstream.

north_star_verdict.headline: Live Shortlisted RFP with a criteria-shaping champion (Andy Simmons) and a clean 6 May demo, but stalled at the integration gate: Andy has been silent since the demo, the NetSuite scoping call he flagged as the next step was never booked, and no economic buyer has engaged on a $150K Best Case deal 72 days from close.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 2025-12-12; ~6.9 months old. Shortlisted since 15 Apr (~82 days in stage). Close 16 Sep = 72 days out. Last genuine two-way buyer exchange was the 6 May demo (~61 days ago); every touch since is unanswered rep outreach (14/20/27 May, 15/26 Jun). To hold 16 Sep the buyer must select by mid-Jul, firm commercials late-Jul, and contract through Aug. A concrete next step (the integration/scoping call) is stuck and the champion is silent, so this is Slowing (not Off Track: rep re-engaged Nicole 26 Jun; not pure Close-Date Risk: a specific action, not just the date, is stalled).
evidence: Next Step 8 May: 'Demo occurred, went well; implementation/integration call is next step' — that call was never booked.
Next Step 30 May: 'Andy has gone quiet. Still pushing.'
Next Step 17 Jun: rep offered a live NetSuite integration — buyer never confirmed.
Next Step 26 Jun: rep multi-threaded to Nicole Gallego (SVP Delivery Ops & IT) — awaiting reply.
No economic buyer engaged in ~7 months; Best Case is indefensible without a named budget owner on a $150K deal 72 days from close.

metrics.narrative: The pain is well articulated but never quantified. Andy Simmons wants to solve the 80% of purchasing activity that happens before a PR is created: on the 6 May demo he said 'the only real controlled portion of the purchase is at the PR creation stage in NetSuite, but 80% of the activity on a lot of purchases occur before that PR gets created. I'm solving for that 80%.' No cost, headcount, cycle-time, or savings baseline was put behind the $150K, so there is no ROI model to hand a budget owner. That gap is more acute given the company is mid-acquisition (Next Step 26 Dec 2025: 'currently acquiring a company'), which puts spend under scrutiny. Move: co-build a quantified case (manual-intake cost, AP cycle-time, sourcing-savings target) Andy can carry to Finance.

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is named or engaged after ~7 months. Andy Simmons runs procurement and controls the evaluation, but he is not the budget owner for a $150K commitment. The most senior Conga person in the deal is Nicole Gallego, Senior VP of Delivery Operations & Information Technology (Salesforce contact role), who Andy introduced on the 6 May demo as 'the lone IT representative, more involved in high level strategy' and who the rep reached to directly on 26 Jun. Finance/CFO sign-off is implied but that person is unnamed. Because Andy owns the RFP, the path to power runs through him or through Nicole, not a cold CFO outreach. This is the single highest-leverage gap and the reason Best Case cannot stand.

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are clear from the RFP and the demo. Bailey Erazo summarized the four buyer-stated priorities on 6 May: 'streamlining intake for all end users,' 'moving Mineral Tree, adding automation' into AP invoicing, 'compliance and visibility around these procurement processes,' and 'time to value / quick wins' with the September go-live. Andy added a fifth explicit test: dynamic, non-linear workflows ('I wanna make sure the workflow is dynamic enough to capture' parallel and sequential tasks, not just approvals). Merlin's agentic negotiation and Teams-native intake landed as differentiators. Confirmed via the issued RFP (13 Apr) and the demo dialogue.
sources: Avoma demo 6 May 2026 (Bailey Erazo, Andy Simmons)

decision_process.narrative: The process is readable from the call sequence: intro 12 Dec 2025, NDA signed ~Jan, 45-question RFP issued 13 Apr, response submitted ~29 Apr, cross-functional demo 6 May bringing procurement (Andy, Craig Adler), Accounts Payable (Ida Stark, Maria Murillo) and IT (Nicole Gallego) into one room, then an intended integration/scoping call that never happened. Andy targeted a decision 'by March' originally (Next Step 26 Dec) and a September go-live, both now slipped. The approval chain above Andy (Finance sign-off for $150K) is unconfirmed. The buyer has run a structured evaluation but the closing steps (selection, integration proof, commercials) are stalled on the buyer.
sources: SF field history 15 Apr 2026 (Formal Evaluation → Shortlisted)

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are early and untouched. A mutual NDA was signed (Next Step 23 Feb: 'Signed NDA'). Implementation is Zycus-delivered, a fit given the constraint Andy raised on the IT function, but no pricing is firmed: the rep gave a wide range in the RFP (Task 29 Apr: 'I gave a large range for the pricing') and has been chasing SaaS and implementation pricing inputs since (Task 14 May: 'SaaS and Implementation Pricing... top of mind'), with no user counts returned. No quote, Order Form, MSA, legal, or InfoSec review has started. Visibility will come once the buyer confirms the phased MVP scope and returns the pricing inputs.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is concrete and owned by Andy Simmons, the new Senior Director of Procurement hired to modernize the function (Next Step 26 Dec: 'Sr Dir of Procurement, New Hire'). Today procurement is uncontrolled before the PR stage, invoices are handled manually in Mineral Tree and NetSuite (Bailey, 6 May: 'invoices are entered into both Mineral Tree and NetSuite'), and an AP team member confirmed the manual drag-and-drop workflow ('we have a general AP inbox... I tend to drop and drag'). Andy wants one controlled front door for the 80% of activity that today happens off-system. Compounding constraint: a phased MVP is required to avoid overloading IT ('so Nicole doesn't have a heart attack with that September go live'). Pain owner is Andy; AP pain owners are Ida Stark and Maria Murillo.
sources: Avoma demo 6 May 2026 (Andy Simmons, AP team)

champion.narrative: Andy Simmons is a developing, now at-risk champion. He authored and issued the RFP, invited Zycus in writing, shaped criteria toward Zycus strengths (full suite, Teams-native intake, agentic negotiation, which he called 'awesome'), and convened procurement, AP and IT for the 6 May demo. What is missing and now at risk: he has been silent since that demo, never confirmed the integration/scoping call or the live NetSuite offer, and never introduced the budget owner. His advocacy is real but unproven at the power level. Because he owns the spec, he is still the path to Finance, so re-arm him rather than route around him, while activating Nicole Gallego as a parallel senior thread.

competition.narrative: The strongest current threat is do-nothing: extend the manual NetSuite process and wait, which becomes the realistic outcome given Andy's silence and mid-acquisition budget scrutiny. No named vendor shortlist has ever been disclosed ('Zycus amongst a few other players,' Next Step 26 Dec 2025). Coupa and Zip surface only from Andy's own usage history at prior employers, not as confirmed Conga bidders, and have faded. A subtle live tension is CLM: Conga is itself a contract-lifecycle vendor, and Zycus defused the overlap on 6 May by positioning integration not replacement ('Conga is going to remain as our central repository... we want the metadata'). Wedge to win: prove live NetSuite integration depth plus a phased MVP that de-risks the September go-live.

deal_movement.summary: Momentum thinned after a strong demo. The 6 May cross-functional demo went well, but the buyer went quiet immediately after; the rep has sent six unanswered follow-ups, pushed the close date from 25 Jun to 16 Sep on 15 Jun, and multi-threaded to Nicole Gallego on 26 Jun. Ball is with Zycus to reopen the relationship via Nicole and Andy.

items.change: Cross-functional demo delivered to procurement, AP and IT; agentic negotiation and Teams intake landed well.
Champion Andy Simmons went silent; six rep follow-ups (14/20/27 May, 15/26 Jun) went unanswered.
Close date pushed from 25 Jun to 16 Sep.
Rep multi-threaded to Nicole Gallego, SVP Delivery Ops & IT, to reopen the deal.

competitive_position.summary: Recency-weighted, the single strongest CURRENT threat is the status quo (extend manual NetSuite, do nothing) given Andy's ~2-month silence and mid-acquisition budget scrutiny. No named vendor shortlist has ever been disclosed. Coupa and Zip come only from Andy's prior-employer usage history, not confirmed Conga bids, and remain faded; Ivalua was a different account and is dormant. Zycus's edge is full intake-to-pay breadth, native Teams UI, agentic negotiation, and vendor-delivered implementation.

competitors.quote: the only real controlled portion of the purchase is at the PR creation stage in NetSuite
competitors.how_we_win: Quantify the cost of the uncontrolled 80% pre-PR spend and manual AP so change is cheaper than standing still through the acquisition.

competitors.how_we_win: Position full intake-to-pay suite plus Merlin autonomous negotiation as deeper than Coupa, with a native Teams front door.

competitors.name: Zip
competitors.quote: when I was at Procore, we did implement intake to procure with Zip
competitors.how_we_win: Zip lacks true eSourcing; lead with eSourcing plus spend-analytics breadth.

competitors.quote: no confirmed mention on a Conga call (seen only on an unrelated 17 Mar touchpoint for a different account)
competitors.source: Avoma touchpoint 17 Mar 2026 (different account, not Conga)
competitors.how_we_win: Not a confirmed Conga competitor; do not over-weight.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Strong functional fit on intake, AP automation and sourcing; the open exposures are supplier-portal adoption, the unproven live NetSuite integration, and whether a phased MVP satisfies the September go-live without over-scoping IT.

best_practice_check.summary: Three levers raise win probability now: break the single-thread onto Nicole, convert Merlin enthusiasm into a funded business case, and beat the do-nothing default with a phased-MVP forcing date.
flags: Break the single-thread: Andy is the only real contact and has gone silent, so activate Nicole Gallego (SVP Delivery Ops & IT), who owns the integration decision, as a parallel senior path to the budget owner.
Convert enthusiasm to a business case: the agentic-negotiation and Merlin intake excitement is unquantified, so build the pre-PR-control and manual-AP cost model that lets Finance fund the $150K.
Beat do-nothing: with mid-acquisition budget scrutiny the real threat is extending NetSuite, so anchor a phased MVP with a concrete September forcing date and live integration proof.

items.name: Nicole Gallego
items.title: Senior VP, Delivery Operations & Information Technology
items.risk: Most senior Conga contact and owner of the integration/IT decision; on the demo and freshly re-contacted 26 Jun, but not yet a committed sponsor; best path to power.

champion_strength.summary: Andy Simmons authored the RFP, shaped criteria toward Zycus and convened the cross-functional demo, but has been silent since 6 May, never introduced the budget owner and never booked the integration call, so his advocacy is developing and now at risk at the power level.
champion_strength.source: SF RFP Invitation tasks 14 Apr 2026; Avoma demo 6 May 2026; SF Next_Step 30 May / 15 Jun 2026

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Merlin's agentic negotiation and Teams-native intake landed strongly on 6 May (Andy: 'I absolutely love the agentic negotiation, that is awesome'), but Merlin is not in the committed product scope and no paid AI workstream is anchored, so the AI value is under-monetized versus the enthusiasm.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS is 5/Moderate. Calls corroborate genuine, buyer-initiated agentic interest (Andy praising autonomous negotiation; Craig asking to deploy an agent in-workflow), but the scope is iContract/iSource/iSupplier with no Merlin module committed, so appetite is real yet not yet paid.

items.detail: No economic buyer or Finance sponsor engaged on a $150K deal after ~7 months; Best Case is indefensible.
Single-threaded on Andy Simmons, who has gone silent; the deal has no confirmed second advocate driving it.
Mid-acquisition spend scrutiny plus a phased-MVP posture signal a defensive, cost-cautious buyer.
items.source: SF Next_Step 26 Dec 2025 + Avoma demo 6 May 2026

items.detail: 16 Sep close requires selection by mid-July, but no selection signal and the integration call is unbooked.
items.source: SF field history 15 Jun 2026 (CloseDate 25 Jun → 16 Sep)

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is cooling: one strong demo, then ~2 months of unanswered rep follow-ups and a stalled integration call. The rep's 26 Jun reach to Nicole is a rep-side touch awaiting a buyer reply, not renewed buyer engagement.

Close date pushed 25 Jun → 16 Sep on 15 Jun with no buyer-confirmed next milestone.
Champion Andy Simmons silent; no economic buyer ever engaged.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Live Shortlisted RFP that Zycus's champion authored and shaped in our favor, with a clean cross-functional demo, but it has stalled: Andy Simmons has been silent for ~2 months, no economic buyer has ever engaged, and the biggest thing between us and the win is reopening the deal through Nicole Gallego and funding a business case before do-nothing wins.

win_position.text: Holds in the mid-50s, not higher, because Shortlisted caps confidence and the champion is now silent with no economic buyer engaged, even though fit, criteria shaping and the demo would otherwise earn more.
Andy Simmons authored the RFP and shaped criteria toward Zycus strengths, praising the agentic negotiation as 'awesome' on the 6 May demo, which keeps us a credible front-runner.
Andy has not replied to six rep follow-ups since 6 May and never introduced the budget owner, so our advocacy is unproven at the power level.
The strongest current threat is do-nothing: mid-acquisition budget scrutiny and a phased-MVP posture make extending manual NetSuite the easy default.

deal_momentum.text: The intended integration/scoping call flagged on 8 May was never booked and the buyer has gone quiet for ~2 months.
The close date was pushed from 25 Jun to 16 Sep on 15 Jun with no buyer-confirmed next milestone.
The rep re-engaged by multi-threading to Nicole Gallego (SVP Delivery Ops & IT) on 26 Jun, a rep-side touch awaiting a buyer reply.

customer_commitment.text: The buyer ran a full RFP, signed an NDA, and brought procurement, AP and IT together for the 6 May demo.
No economic buyer has engaged in ~7 months and the pricing inputs (user counts) were never returned, so commercial commitment is unproven.

deal_risk.text: Single-threaded on a silent champion with an unnamed Finance decision maker on a $150K Best Case deal 72 days from close.
Mid-acquisition budget scrutiny plus a defensive phased-MVP stance raise the risk of a no-decision outcome.

momentum_stall.evidence: Integration call never booked; six unanswered rep follow-ups since 6 May; close date slipped.

eb_absent.evidence: No economic buyer engaged in ~7 months on a $150K deal.

champion_shaped_rfp.evidence: Andy Simmons authored the RFP and shaped criteria toward Zycus.

scope_change.to: iContract;iSource;iSupplier full intake-to-pay (phased: MVP NetSuite + AP integration in September, deeper integrations later)
scope_change.detail: Product scope is unchanged, but the buyer has adopted a defensive phased-MVP posture ('a minimum viable product in September and building deeper integrations in a phased approach') driven by IT capacity limits and mid-acquisition budget caution.

customer_preference.evidence: Andy Simmons on 6 May: 'I absolutely love the agentic negotiation, that is awesome' and probed deep Zycus-specific workflow questions, but went silent afterward with no decisive lean or commercial next step.

business_case.evidence: Rich qualitative pain (uncontrolled pre-PR spend, manual AP) but no cost, cycle-time or savings numbers were put behind the $150K.

contributions.evidence: Next Step carries 12 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.
Stage is ahead of the engagement/evidence the sweep found.

commentary.win_position: Win 53 is carried by preference strength +0.40 (weight 20); champion strength +0.30 (weight 15). Offsetting it: momentum 52 vs stage-expected 56.
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 52 (roughly flat): Next Step carries 12 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.deal_risk: Risk 32 is driven by Stage is ahead of the engagement/evidence the sweep found; Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 29 is anchored on the stage win (53), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 52, and dragged by risk 32. Read is Partial Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Partial Read: 4 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, stage integrity, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.

cro_panel.intro: Live Shortlisted RFP that Zycus's champion authored and shaped in our favor, with a clean cross-functional demo, but it has stalled: Andy Simmons has been silent for ~2 months, no economic buyer has ever engaged, and the biggest thing between us and the win is reopening the deal through Nicole Gallego and funding a business case before do-nothing wins.
